key_requirements: Phase 1 implementation that Phase 2 directly extends — defines spec_alignment module structure, violation model, CheckResult, CLI pattern
Explicit scope boundary: 'Phase 1 only — no code↔spec coverage (Phase 2 #1141)'
Data models SpecDocument, SpecSection, CodeBlock, Violation, ViolationKind, FileResult, CheckResult are the base types Phase 2 extends
Architecture: single spec_alignment::check() entry point, files under crates/cclab-sdd/src/spec_alignment/
CLI: cclab sdd check-alignment [path] --json, exit 0/1
CLI command tree — Phase 2 extends check-alignment with --coverage flag
CLI handler pattern: cclab-sdd-cli/src/check_alignment.rs delegates to spec_alignment::check()
CLI → logic mapping table needs new row for coverage sub-mode
Defines symbol kinds (fn_item, struct_item, trait_item, impl_item) extracted by daemon's symbol index
Phase 2 queries the daemon's pre-built symbol index for these symbol types to perform coverage mapping
Understanding of symbol scoping (function body, struct fields, impl methods) needed for annotation matching
Multi-language AST analysis pipeline (Python, TypeScript, Rust) using tree-sitter — same infrastructure as daemon
Phase 2 scans source files for @spec annotations across all three languages
Fast-path (AST-only) pattern: Phase 2 uses direct parsing for annotation extraction without LLM enrichment
Daemon index stored at {project_root}/cclab/.index/ — socket at {root}/cclab/.index/daemon.sock
DaemonClient::for_workspace(root) constructs client from storage::resolve_socket_path()
Phase 2 calls DaemonClient::symbols(file) over the Unix socket to get symbol names/kinds/lines for coverage mapping
IndexStatus.is_ready check needed before coverage analysis runs
Workflow context: Phase 2 is a standalone library/CLI feature — workflow integration is Phase 3 (#1142)
SDD phase states reference for where check-alignment will eventually be called
resolve_spec_dir() used to canonicalize spec paths from @spec annotations against cclab/specs/ root
SddConfig.specs.scopes map helps validate that @spec annotation paths reference real spec directories
